subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **50 firework cards** in 5 colors (White, Red, Blue, Yellow, Green):
  - Each color: three 1s, two 2s, two 3s, two 4s, one 5
- **8 blue clock tokens** (information tokens)
- **3 red fuse tokens** (mistake tokens)
- **5 multicolor cards** (variant/expansion)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle all 50 cards.**
2. **Deal cards** — Hold cards facing AWAY from you (you cannot see your own hand).
   - 2-3 players: 5 cards each
   - 4-5 players: 4 cards each
3. **Place 8 blue tokens** (clock side up) and **3 red tokens** in the center.
4. **Leave space** for 5 firework piles (one per color).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Hold cards outward** — You can see everyone else's cards but NOT your own.
- **No revealing** — You cannot look at your own cards or reveal them accidentally.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Hanabi is a cooperative card game where players build 5 firework displays (one per color) from 1 to 5. The twist: you can't see your own cards. Teammates give limited hints to help you play the right cards.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do exactly ONE action:

1. **Give a hint** — Spend 1 blue token to tell another player about their cards.
2. **Play a card** — Place a card from your hand onto a firework pile.
3. **Discard a card** — Discard a card to regain 1 blue token.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Giving Hints
- Flip 1 blue token to its used side.
- Tell one other player about ALL cards in their hand of one specific color OR one specific number.
- You MUST point to every card that matches.
- Example: "These two cards are blue" or "This card is a 3."
- Cannot give a hint that applies to zero cards.

#### Playing Cards
- Choose a card from your hand (without looking) and place it on the corresponding firework pile.
- **Success** — If the card is the next number needed (e.g., Blue 3 on a Blue pile showing 1-2), it stays.
- **Bonus** — Completing a firework (playing the 5) returns 1 blue token.
- **Failure** — If the card doesn't fit, it's discarded and 1 red fuse token is lost.

#### Discarding
- Remove a card from your hand (without looking at it first) to the discard pile.
- Reclaim 1 blue token (flip from used to available).
- Draw a new card from the deck.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **3 fuses = game over** — If all 3 red tokens are lost, the fireworks explode and everyone loses.
- **Card uniqueness** — Each 5 exists only once per color; discarding a 5 makes that firework impossible to complete.
- **Hint completeness** — When giving a hint, you must indicate ALL matching cards.
- **No blue tokens** — If all 8 are used, you cannot give hints until someone discards.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### End Triggers
- All 3 fuse tokens are lost (score = 0).
- All 5 fireworks are completed (perfect score = 25).
- The deck runs out — each player gets 1 final turn.

#### Scoring
- Sum the highest card played in each of the 5 firework piles.
- **Maximum** 25 points (all five piles completed 1-5).
- **Rating**: 0-5 horrible, 6-10 mediocre, 11-15 honorable, 16-20 excellent, 21-24 amazing, 25 legendary.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Prioritize 1s** — Get all five 1s played early to unlock the fireworks.
- **Hint about playable cards** — Focus hints on cards that can be played immediately.
- **Don't discard unknowns** — If you have no info about a card, it might be critical.
- **Watch the discard pile** — Track which cards are gone to know what's still safe to play.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Conventions** — Groups develop hint conventions (e.g., hinting a number means "play this").
- **Finesse plays** — Advanced technique: give a hint to one player that indirectly tells another what to play.
- **Discard order** — Discard from the oldest/rightmost card if no information has been given about it.
- **Save 5s** — Hint about 5s immediately to prevent accidental discards.
- **Token management** — Balance hint-giving with discarding to maintain blue token supply.
